ops.title: Workflow tool may deliver args JSON-stringified to plan-trip.js (scriptPath invocations) — validation throws 'script must be an absolute path'
ops.body:
Observed 2026-07-13: launching `skills/getaway/plan-trip.js` via `Workflow({scriptPath, args})` delivered `args` as a JSON **string**, twice reproducibly, even when passed as a proper object — so `const a = args` saw a string, `a.script` was undefined, and validation threw `plan-trip: script must be an absolute path to getaway.sh` instantly (workflow.js:95, 0 agents).

**Diagnosis path**: the ABS_PATH regex passes the path (verified with node), so the throw means `typeof a.script !== 'string'` → args wasn't an object.

**Workaround**: copy the script to scratch and change line ~110 to `const a = typeof args === "string" ? JSON.parse(args) : args;`, then relaunch. Do NOT commit that shim — the repo bans defensive coding, and pre-hybrid invocations of the same file worked in earlier sessions, so this is likely a harness regression. Recorded as a cc-notes papercut too. If getaway skill runs start failing at plan-trip validation with this exact error, this is why.
